text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Marcus T. Delgado
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: 1965
SEX: Male
ORDERING CLINICIAN: Medical Oncology / Interventional Radiology
PROCEDURE: CT-Guided Core Needle Biopsy, Right Lower Lobe Pulmonary Nodule
SPECIMEN DESIGNATION: A — Right Lower Lobe Lung, Core Needle Biopsy (CT-guided)
DIAGNOSING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGNATURE: Electronically signed
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICAL HISTORY:
Mr. Delgado is a 58-year-old male with a known history of Stage IV (cT3 N2b M1b) moderately differentiated adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon, initially diagnosed with synchronous bilobar hepatic metastases and KRAS G12D mutation confirmed on comprehensive NGS. He was treated with FOLFOX plus bevacizumab for 12 cycles (including conversion to 5-FU/leucovorin/bevacizumab after oxaliplatin discontinuation for grade 3 peripheral neuropathy), followed by maintenance 5-fluorouracil/leucovorin/bevacizumab. A recent restaging CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis identified two new pulmonary nodules not present on prior imaging: a right lower lobe nodule measuring 1.1 cm and a right upper lobe nodule measuring 0.7 cm. Given the clinical context of known metastatic colorectal carcinoma with disease progression on maintenance therapy (rising CEA from 79 to 142 ng/mL), CT-guided biopsy of the dominant right lower lobe nodule was performed to confirm the etiology of the pulmonary findings and guide further systemic therapy decisions.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFICATION AND ADEQUACY:

Specimen A: Right lower lobe lung, CT-guided core needle biopsy
  — Three core needle biopsy fragments received in formalin, each measuring approximately 1.2–1.5 cm in length and 0.1 cm in diameter. Cores are tan-white and firm. ADEQUATE FOR DIAGNOSIS.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A is received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number, and designated "right lower lobe lung biopsy." The container holds three cylindrical core needle biopsy tissue fragments. The cores range from 1.2 to 1.5 cm in length and approximately 0.1 cm in greatest diameter. The tissue is tan-white to pale yellow, firm, and rubbery in consistency. Cut surfaces reveal homogeneous tan-white tissue without grossly visible hemorrhage, necrosis, or cyst formation on inspection, though microscopic necrosis is identified on histologic sections. All tissue is entirely submitted for histologic processing.

Cassette A1: All three cores, entirely submitted.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Histologic sections of the core needle biopsy reveal fragments of lung parenchyma with near-complete effacement by a moderately differentiated infiltrating adenocarcinoma. The neoplastic process is characterized by irregular, angulated glandular structures and tubular formations embedded within a desmoplastic stromal background. The glands vary in size and shape, with some exhibiting back-to-back crowding and focal cribriform architecture. A subset of glands demonstrates central luminal "dirty necrosis" — the hallmark intraluminal granular eosinophilic necrotic debris that is characteristically associated with colorectal adenocarcinoma — a feature readily identified on multiple sections. No lepidic, acinar (primary pulmonary), papillary, or micropapillary architectural patterns typically associated with primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma are identified.

The tumor cells are columnar to cuboidal in shape, with elongated pseudostratified hyperchromatic nuclei displaying coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and variable mitotic activity (approximately 6–8 mitoses per 10 high-power fields). Nuclear pleomorphism is moderate. The cytoplasm is pale eosinophilic to amphophilic; no significant intracytoplasmic mucin accumulation is identified to suggest a mucinous or signet ring cell variant. Rare scattered tumor cells with slightly more open, vesicular nuclei are present, but signet ring cells are not identified.

The adjacent lung parenchyma shows focal entrapped alveolar architecture at the periphery of the biopsy cores, with mild reactive pneumocyte hyperplasia. No features of primary pulmonary malignancy, granulomatous inflammation, or organizing pneumonia are identified in the residual lung parenchyma.

Lymphovascular invasion is suspected focally based on neoplastic glands adjacent to thin-walled vascular structures, though definitive assessment is limited on core biopsy material.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. RIGHT LOWER LOBE LUNG, CT-GUIDED CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   — METASTATIC ADENOCARCINOMA, CONSISTENT WITH COLORECTAL PRIMARY
   — Morphologic features including glandular architecture with intraluminal dirty necrosis and absence of lepidic or primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma patterns are characteristic of metastatic colorectal origin
   — See ancillary studies below

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Specimen A; block A1):
Antigen               Clone        Result
----------------------------------------------
CK20                  Ks20.8       POSITIVE (diffuse, strong cytoplasmic)
CDX2                  CDX2-88      POSITIVE (diffuse, strong nuclear)
SATB2                 EP281        POSITIVE (strong nuclear, >80% of tumor cells)
CK7                   OV-TL 12/30  NEGATIVE
TTF-1                 8G7G3/1      NEGATIVE
Napsin A              IP64         NEGATIVE
p40                   BC28         NEGATIVE
p63                   4A4          NEGATIVE
Synaptophysin         SY38         NEGATIVE
Chromogranin A        DAK-A3       NEGATIVE

Interpretation: The immunophenotype — CK20 positive, CDX2 positive, SATB2 positive, CK7 negative, TTF-1 negative — is characteristic of metastatic colorectal adenocarcinoma. This immunoprofile effectively excludes primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma (which would be expected to show TTF-1 and/or Napsin A positivity with CK7 expression) as well as neuroendocrine carcinoma and squamous cell carcinoma. The combined positivity for CK20, CDX2, and SATB2 is highly specific for colorectal origin.

MOLECULAR / TARGETED PCR:
KRAS Codon 12/13 Mutational Analysis (targeted real-time PCR, performed on FFPE tissue, Specimen A block A1):
   — KRAS p.G12D (c.35G>A) MUTATION DETECTED
   — Variant allele frequency: approximately 38% (consistent with expected tumor cellularity on this core biopsy specimen)
   — This result is CONCORDANT with the known KRAS G12D mutation identified in the sigmoid colon primary tumor and previously reported on comprehensive NGS (see prior molecular report).

MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MMR IHC, pending at time of initial sign-out; to be reported as addendum):
   — MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2 immunohistochemistry ordered on Specimen A; results PENDING.
   — Note: MMR-proficient (MSS) status was established on the primary sigmoid colonic biopsy; concordant status is anticipated, though repeat testing is performed per institutional protocol for newly biopsied metastatic sites.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:

The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings in this right lower lobe lung core needle biopsy are diagnostic of metastatic adenocarcinoma with an origin from the colorectum. The combination of glandular architecture with intraluminal dirty necrosis, strong and diffuse CK20, CDX2, and SATB2 positivity, with negativity for CK7, TTF-1, and Napsin A, effectively establishes this as a colorectal metastasis rather than a primary pulmonary neoplasm. Furthermore, the detection of the identical KRAS p.G12D driver mutation (VAF 38%) in this biopsy specimen is concordant with the patient's known primary sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma (KRAS G12D VAF 42% on primary tumor NGS), providing additional molecular confirmation of metastatic origin.

These findings, taken in the context of the clinical history of Stage IV sigmoid colon adenocarcinoma with known hepatic metastases and interval development of bilateral pulmonary nodules on surveillance imaging, support the radiographic impression of pulmonary metastatic disease from colorectal carcinoma.

MMR IHC results will be reported as an addendum upon completion.

Correlation with clinical, radiographic, and prior molecular findings is recommended.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ADDENDUM (to be issued upon completion of pending studies):
   — MMR IHC (MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2): PENDING — results to follow

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIFFERENTIAL DIAGNOSIS CONSIDERED:
   1. Primary pulmonary adenocarcinoma — EXCLUDED by negative TTF-1, negative Napsin A, negative CK7, and absence of lepidic/acinar/papillary/micropapillary lung adenocarcinoma architecture; positive CK20, CDX2, SATB2 inconsistent
   2. Metastatic adenocarcinoma from upper gastrointestinal primary (gastric, pancreatic, biliary) — Less likely; CK7-negative, CDX2-positive, SATB2-positive profile most strongly supports colorectal origin over upper GI; concordant KRAS G12D mutation confirms colorectal primary
   3. Metastatic mucinous adenocarcinoma — Considered given focal mucinous features described in primary; however, prominent mucinous component not identified in this metastatic specimen
   4. Carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor — Excluded by negative synaptophysin and chromogranin A

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PHOTOGRAPHS: None (core needle biopsy)
INTRAOPERATIVE CONSULTATION: Not performed
DECAL: Not applicable
SPECIAL STAINS: Not performed (IHC sufficient for diagnosis)
ELECTRON MICROSCOPY: Not performed

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Report finalized by: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Case reviewed with [Thoracic Pathology Fellow / Resident]
Addendum pending: MMR IHC results
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
